title: A wedding procession I walked into in Cairo
summary: A short story about a wedding parade you stumbled into in a foreign city.
topic: A wedding procession I walked into in Cairo
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a parade, wedding, or procession you accidentally walked into abroad. Describe the music, the people, and how you reacted. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: I was walking back to my hotel near Tahrir when…
Suddenly the whole street filled with…
An older woman pulled my arm and said…
sampleText: I was walking back to my hotel near Tahrir Square in Cairo at about ten o'clock at night. I had eaten too much kushari and wanted to clear my head before sleeping. Suddenly the whole street filled with bright headlights, drums, and a long line of cars honking their horns in rhythm. A wedding procession was coming straight toward me, and there was nowhere to step aside because the pavement was full of plastic chairs. I stood very still against a closed shop window, trying to look polite and small. An older woman with a yellow scarf saw me, smiled, and pulled my arm. 'Yalla, dance!' she said. The bride was sitting in a white car at the centre of the procession, waving at everyone. I clapped along badly while the drums were still beating around me. A small boy gave me a sweet wrapped in pink paper. I did not know anyone, but for ten minutes I belonged to that family. When the procession turned the corner, the street became dark and quiet again. I walked the last three blocks to my hotel with my hand still sticky from the sweet. Some cities welcome you without asking first.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Tôi đang đi về khách sạn

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple for sudden events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I was walking back to my hotel... Suddenly the whole street filled with...' — past continuous tả việc đang diễn ra, past simple tả sự kiện bất ngờ chen vào.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I was walking back to my hotel... Suddenly the whole street filled with...' — past continuous for what was happening; past simple for the sudden event that broke in.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for an earlier cause
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had eaten too much kushari and wanted to clear my head' — past perfect chỉ nguyên nhân (đã ăn quá nhiều) xảy ra trước hành động chính (muốn đi dạo).
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had eaten too much kushari and wanted to clear my head' — past perfect signals the earlier cause (over-eating) before the main past action (wanting to walk).

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi đang đi về khách sạn gần Quảng trường Tahrir ở Cairo lúc khoảng mười giờ tối. Tôi đã ăn quá nhiều kushari và muốn đi dạo cho thoáng đầu trước khi ngủ. Đột nhiên cả con phố ngập trong ánh đèn pha sáng, tiếng trống, và một hàng dài xe bấm còi theo nhịp. Một đoàn rước dâu đang tiến thẳng về phía tôi, và không có chỗ nào để tránh sang một bên vì vỉa hè đầy ghế nhựa. Tôi đứng yên áp vào một ô cửa kính tiệm đã đóng, cố tỏ ra lịch sự và nhỏ bé. Một người phụ nữ lớn tuổi quàng khăn vàng thấy tôi, mỉm cười, và kéo tay tôi. 'Yalla, nhảy đi!' bà nói. Cô dâu đang ngồi trong một chiếc xe trắng ở giữa đoàn rước, vẫy tay với tất cả mọi người. Tôi vỗ tay theo một cách vụng về trong khi tiếng trống vẫn đang vang quanh tôi. Một cậu bé đưa cho tôi một viên kẹo gói giấy hồng. Tôi không biết ai cả, nhưng trong mười phút, tôi thuộc về gia đình ấy. Khi đoàn rước rẽ góc, con phố trở nên tối và yên tĩnh lại. Tôi đi nốt ba khối phố cuối về khách sạn với bàn tay vẫn dính từ viên kẹo. Có những thành phố chào đón bạn mà không hỏi trước.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past continuous opening.
options: I was walking back to my hotel near Tahrir Square in Cairo.
I walking back to my hotel near Tahrir Square in Cairo.
I walked back to my hotel near Tahrir Square in Cairo at ten o'clock at night.
I am walking back to my hotel near Tahrir Square in Cairo.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous sets the scene for a sudden inserted event.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past perfect sentence.
options: I had eaten too much kushari.
I have ate too much kushari.
I had ate too much kushari.
I have eaten too much kushari before.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect 'had eaten' for an action completed earlier than the main past timeline.

options: a line of people or vehicles moving together as part of a ceremony
a small private meeting
a kind of meal
a road sign

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about cars honking.
options: a long line of cars honking their horns in rhythm
a long line of cars honked their horns in rhythm
a long line of cars honking they horns in rhythm
a long line of cars are honking their horns in rhythm
mcQuestions.explanation: Present participle 'honking' as a reduced relative clause modifying 'cars'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct past continuous sentence.
options: The bride was sitting in a white car.
The bride sitting in a white car.
The bride is sitting in a white car.
The bride sat in a white car was.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'was sitting' for an ongoing past action.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'pull my arm' mean here?
options: to grab the arm and tug it gently
to push the arm away
to pat the arm
to point at the arm
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pull (someone's) arm' = a gentle tug to lead or invite.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence with 'try to'.
options: I stood very still against a closed shop window, trying to look polite and small.
I stood very still against a closed shop window, trying look polite and small.
I stood very still against a closed shop window, trying for looking polite and small.
I stood very still against a closed shop window, try to look polite and small.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Try to + base verb'; present participle 'trying' as adverbial.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'while' + past continuous.
options: I clapped along badly while the drums were still beating around me.
I clapped along badly while the drums still beating around me.
I clapped along badly while the drums still beat around me.
I was clapped along badly while the drums beat around me.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous 'were beating' for ongoing background; past simple 'clapped' for the action.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'belong to' mean in 'I belonged to that family'?
options: to feel that you are part of a group
to own the family
to be related by blood
to live in the same building
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Belong to' (emotional) = feel a sense of membership in a group.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence about the procession leaving.
options: When the procession turned the corner, the street became dark and quiet again.
When the procession turn the corner, the street became dark and quiet again.
When the procession turned the corner, the street become dark and quiet again.
When the procession turning the corner, the street became dark and quiet again.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses in past simple ('turned', 'became') — two finished sequential events.

options: Some cities welcome you without asking first.
Some cities welcome you without to ask first.
Some cities welcomes you without asking first.
Some cities are welcome you without asking first.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural subject 'cities' → 'welcome'; 'without + -ing' is the fixed pattern.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When you walk by accident into someone else's celebration in another country, are you a guest or an intruder? What changes that?
